agent_final_filter.verdict: Chain incomplete: WMI-4 (control-flow hijack) has zero confirmed candidates and no authoritative object/nucleus was identified (chain_core=[], authoritative_objects=None), so the 4-WMI chain cannot be closed despite strong WMI-1/WMI-2 evidence and confirmed WMI-3 attacker-content sites.

kept.func: unresolved (WMI-3 bucket, no site_oracle detail supplied)
kept.reason: One of only 3 SE-confirmed WMI-3 (attacker-controlled content) sites in the whole sweep; this is the sole confirmation available for a role the chain cannot be completed without, so it is kept even though no nucleus/WMI-4 co-location was established to anchor it structurally.

kept.func: unresolved (WMI-3 bucket, no site_oracle detail supplied)
kept.reason: Same as cand126 — part of the only 3-member WMI-3 set; kept as necessary-role evidence, not as a confirmed nucleus member.

kept.func: unresolved (WMI-3 bucket, no site_oracle detail supplied)
kept.reason: Same as cand126/cand128 — highest-numbered candidate in the WMI-3 set (cand132 of 133 oracles); kept as necessary-role evidence pending a confirmed WMI-4/nucleus link.

dropped.reason: No authoritative object was ever identified (authoritative_objects=None, chain_core=[]), so none of these can be shown to be 'on the nucleus' or 'feeding the nucleus'. With 33 independent WMI-1 confirmations, no single one is uniquely required to establish the role either, so none satisfies any keep criterion; the role itself remains unanchored to a concrete struct.

dropped.func: unresolved (WMI-2 bucket, no site_oracle detail supplied)
dropped.reason: Same reasoning as the WMI-1 bucket: no confirmed nucleus exists to test dataflow against, and 42 independent confirmations mean no single finding is the sole evidence for the live-resource-persist role, so none meets the keep criteria.

dropped.cand: remaining 55 oracles not present in any WMI-1/2/3/4 confirmed list
dropped.reason: Not SE-confirmed for any WMI role (discarded/errored or never reached by the native-replay filter per the SE Sweep Summary); no basis to keep.

agent_final_filter.chain_summary: Of 133 oracles, 78 survived native-replay confirmation, split WMI-1=33, WMI-2=42, WMI-3=3, WMI-4=0. WMI-4 (control-flow hijack through attacker content) has no confirmed evidence at all, and the co-occurrence anchor never resolved an authoritative object (chain_core empty, authoritative_objects=None). Per the necessary-condition rule, chain_complete cannot be true, so wma_confirmed=false. Only the 3 WMI-3 candidates are kept as the sole surviving evidence for the attacker-content role; all WMI-1/WMI-2 findings are dropped for lacking any confirmed structural link to a nucleus, and the remaining 55 oracles were never SE-confirmed at all. This finding set documents strong signal-divergence and live-resource-persistence behavior but does not close the chain into a demonstrated weird machine.

oracle_spec.assertion: dmamove_token_slot.tag stays within the domain its consumer's agreement governs
assumptions: producer sets tag without enforcing the consumer's cross-field invariant

oracle_spec.assertion: dmamove_token_slot.generation is interpreted consistently across all live consumers
assumptions: resource stays live while read under >= 2 incompatible models

oracle_spec.assertion: file holds trusted, not attacker-determined, content
assumptions: externally-originated content installed into the field

oracle_spec.assertion: pending_descs governs a bounded, trusted referenced operand
assumptions: access extent/source/dest loaded from the field
